name: Entrance Transition
paraphrase: Make a clear transition space at the entrance of every building, marked by a change of light, sound, direction, surface, level, or enclosure, so that the person arriving is psychologically prepared to leave the street behind and enter the more private realm of the house. Alexander holds that without this transition the inside of the house is never truly experienced as a distinct world, because the visitor crosses the threshold without any felt change of state.
